content.text: Hey Guilherme! How can I help you today?

message.content:
<messages>
<message sender="Guilherme Barros" time="2026-02-22T18:26:15.000Z">What capabilities do you have?</message>
</messages>

content.text:
Hey Guilherme! I'm Andy, your personal assistant. Here's what I can do for you:

*Core Capabilities:*
• Answer questions and have conversations
• Search the web and fetch content from URLs
• Browse the web interactively (open pages, click buttons, fill forms, take screenshots, extract data)
• Read and write files in my workspace
• Run bash commands and scripts
• Work with code and help with programming tasks

*Scheduling & Automation:*
• Schedule one-time reminders
• Set up recurring tasks (daily, weekly, custom intervals)
• Run automated tasks in the background

*Memory & Context:*
• Remember information from our past conversations
• Keep track of your preferences and important details
• Access conversation history to recall previous discussions

*WhatsApp Groups (Admin):*
• Since this is the main channel, I can manage other WhatsApp groups
• Register new groups so I can respond there too
• Schedule tasks for specific groups

Is there something specific you'd like me to help you with?

content.text:
Yes! I have full access to my own codebase. This is a Node.js/TypeScript project called "nanoclaw" - a WhatsApp bot powered by Claude (Anthropic's AI).

*What I can see:*
• The entire source code in `/workspace/project/src/`
• Configuration files and setup scripts
• The message database (`/workspace/project/store/messages.db`)
• Group configurations and memory files
• Skills engine that extends my capabilities
• Container setup for isolated group environments

*What this means:*
• I can read and understand how I work under the hood
• I can explain my architecture and implementation
• I could potentially help debug or explain issues
• I can see the data structures, APIs, and integrations

Would you like me to explain any specific part of how I work? For example:
• How messages are processed
• How scheduling works
• How the group isolation system works
• The Claude SDK integration
• The MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools
